title: QUIC Interop Runner Multiconnect Sequential Dispatch Verification
verification_method: Local execution, artifact inspection, and report review.
preconditions: The local host can resolve and bind loopback addresses.
The test host can create temporary PEM files and qlog directories.
The managed QUIC runtime is available in the current .NET environment.
scope: Verify that the `multiconnect` testcase uses the real endpoint-host transfer path sequentially, one managed connection per URL, and that malformed input fails before transport success is claimed.
procedure: Run the new requirement-home test file or a narrow filter that selects `REQ_QUIC_INT_0015`.
Inspect the captured stdout, stderr, and qlog artifacts from the sequential multiconnect smoke run.
Confirm the output shows connection 1/2 and 2/2 markers instead of a generic unsupported exit.
Confirm malformed multiconnect input fails before transport success is claimed and does not produce qlog output.
If the tree is temporarily mid-edit and a run fails for an unrelated build reason, wait briefly and rerun once to confirm the lane still behaves honestly after the transient issue clears.
expected_result: The local interop harness routes `multiconnect` through the real endpoint-host transfer path one URL at a time, the sequential smoke run completes with qlog artifacts, and malformed input fails early without broadening the supported promise.
status_summary: Passed; the requirement-home test lane confirmed sequential `multiconnect` dispatch, qlog capture, and malformed-input rejection on 2026-04-20.
